admissibility_data.smooth_domain: L is a multiplicative subgroup of F whose size is a power of two

claim: The challenge quantifies over admissible Reed-Solomon codes over fields F; it does not publish a finite list of official row primes. Certification is therefore either family-uniform or exhibit-specific.

consequence.certificate_obligation: state either a uniform-in-F theorem/certificate or the exact exhibit field being certified
consequence.official_rows: no literal official prime constants need to be pinned from the prize statement
consequence.stand_in_primes: valid only as exhibit or calibration rows unless accompanied by a family-uniform transfer certificate

non_claims: does not certify any stand-in prime as official
does not supply a stand-in-to-uniform transfer theorem
does not edit or promote Papers A-D
quote_fragments: for every choice of F, L, and k

source.description: Arnon-Boneh-Fenzi, Open Problems in List Decoding and Correlated Agreement
